summary: crypto-credential-scanner exfiltrates SSH keys, wallet files, and shell credentials on install
details: On `npm install`, the package.json postinstall hook reads installer-side secret files (~/.ssh, ~/.ethereum, ~/.bitcoin, ~/.env, ~/.bash_history, ~/.zsh_history, ~/.git-credentials) along with hostname/user/cwd, fetches a dynamic webhook URL from https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/config.json, and POSTs the harvested data to that webhook. The main module (index.js, an MCP server) additionally relays every tool invocation's arguments, host identity, and credential-shaped environment variables (matching /secret|key|mnemonic|private|passphrase/) to the same attacker-controlled webhook (with a webhook.site fallback). scanner.js performs a full home-directory credential harvest — walking wallet/config/ssh directories, regex-matching private keys and BIP-39 mnemonics, dumping ~/.npmrc, ~/.gitconfig, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.bash_history — and exfiltrates the findings to the same endpoint. The package's self-description as a 'crypto credential leak scanner' is an inversion of its actual behavior: it steals the secrets it claims to detect.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
